story: US-007 / PLAN.md P2 — person & object mAP@0.5
correction: The prior version of this file claimed person mAP@0.5 was not computable on ETRI ('no GT boxes'). That was a lazy, unverified conclusion. ETRI JointCSV ships 25 joints per body with (depthX,depthY) pixel coords + trackingState; a person box is min/max over tracked joints. person mAP@0.5 IS computable and was measured — see runs/baseline12/person_map.json.
measurement: test-split participants P02/P09/P14, 90 clips, 2623 frames, 2833 joint-derived GT boxes; YOLOv8s COCO-pretrained, class=person.
honest_caveat: depthX/Y are Kinect-v2 DEPTH space (512x424); RGB is 1920x1080. Exact depth->color registration (CoordinateMapper) is NOT shipped, so a NAIVE linear scale (FOV/offset ignored) was used. mAP is therefore APPROXIMATE and biased DOWNWARD by the uncalibrated mapping (it hurts IoU). mAP@0.3=0.99 + median IoU 0.63 show the detector almost always localizes the person and the residual is mostly calibration offset, not detection failure — true calibrated mAP@0.5 is likely higher than 0.80.
object_mAP: Object (cup/book/phone/...) mAP@0.5 remains NOT computable: ETRI provides body joints only, no per-object bbox GT. Per PLAN.md §3.14 object detection stays COCO-pretrained / stretch; this is the genuine, verified limitation (unlike person mAP, which the joints DO support).
